_comment: Cisco ASA (Secure Firewall ASA) syslog connection-event field inventory, scored against OCSF Network Activity (4001). Unlike PAN-OS's fixed CSV, ASA emits message-ID-keyed semi-structured text; this inventory is the UNION of documented fields across the connection-event message family that carries the network 5-tuple: Built/Teardown TCP (302013/302014), UDP (302015/302016), ICMP (302020/302021), SCTP (302303-302306), and ACL Deny (106023/106100). Each field is a documented element of one or more of those messages; fields specific to one message (duration/bytes/teardown_reason on Teardown; acl_id on Deny; icmp_type/code on ICMP) are noted. Audit/VPN/system messages (the other ~2000 ASA message IDs) are out of scope — they route to other OCSF classes. Transcribed from the Cisco Secure Firewall ASA Series Syslog Messages guide; corroborated against Elastic Filebeat cisco module and the Splunk TA. See PROVENANCE.md.

fields.note: ASA message number (302013/302014/106023/...); the event discriminator
ASA/syslog severity 0-7 embedded in the %ASA-N- tag
Built / Teardown / Deny (conflates connection lifecycle and disposition)
inbound / outbound (which side initiated the control connection)
TCP / UDP / ICMP / SCTP or IP protocol number
AAA / identity-firewall (idfw) user associated with the connection
total bytes transferred over the connection (Teardown); single total, NOT directional
TCP FINs / TCP Reset / SYN Timeout / Connection timeout / ... (Teardown messages)
access-group / ACL name that denied the packet (106023/106100)
